title: Albania best beaches by car: Explore the Riviera from Tirana Airport
excerpt: Discover the top seven Albanian summer beaches you can reach by car after landing at Tirana Rinas airport, with practical driving tips and route details.
intro: You’ve just landed at Tirana Rinas (TIA) and the Mediterranean sun is calling. With a rental car from AutoConnect, the Albanian Riviera’s crystal‑clear coves are only a few hours away. Here’s a practical road‑trip guide to the seven best summer beaches you can reach by car.

sections.title: Albania best beaches by car: an overview of the Riviera
sections.body: The Albanian Riviera stretches over roughly 300 km of coastline south of Tirana, linked by the SH4 and SH8 highways. The primary gateway is the Llogara Pass, about 130 km from the airport, offering spectacular views before the road winds down to the sea. Driving the route lets you discover Albania best beaches by car, each with its own character – from the pebbly coves near Himarë to the wide sandy bays of Ksamil. A reliable rental from AutoConnect makes the journey smooth and flexible.

sections.title: From Tirana to Dhermi – 3‑hour coastal drive
sections.body: Dhermi lies roughly 160 km (about 3 hours) from TIA. After passing the Llogara Pass, the road follows the cliff‑side, offering continuous sea views. A small paid parking lot sits at the edge of the village, putting you just steps from the turquoise water and the lively promenade. The drive is mostly paved, but some sections are narrow and steep, so keep a safe distance from on‑coming traffic, especially during the summer rush.

sections.title: Jale and Gjipe – hidden gems near Himarë
sections.body: Jale Beach, about 170 km (≈3 h) from the airport, is a pebble‑cove framed by olive groves. A modest parking area leads directly onto the sand, and the town of Himarë offers several cafés for a quick break. Further along, Gjipe Beach is 180 km (≈3.5 h) away; the road ends at a small lot, then a 20‑minute hike through a canyon brings you to the secluded bay. Both spots are perfect for travelers who enjoy a bit of adventure after the drive.

sections.title: Ksamil, Sarandë and the southern coves
sections.body: Ksamil’s sandy beaches, including Mirror Beach, sit about 210 km (≈4 h) from Tirana. The town provides free public parking near the centre, making beach access effortless. Sarandë, the main gateway to the southern coast, is roughly 240 km (≈4 h) away and serves as a base for day trips to nearby coves such as Bunec and Lukova. The final stretch of the SH8 is well‑maintained, but occasional narrow sections require careful maneuvering, especially in winter when weather can change quickly.

tip.title: Pro Tip: Time your Llogara Pass crossing
tip.body: Leave Tirana early in the morning (around 7 am) to beat the tourist traffic and enjoy clearer mountain visibility. The pass can become congested in summer afternoons, and the steep sections are easier to navigate with cooler temperatures.

faq.question: How long does it take to drive from Tirana Airport to the main beaches of the Albanian Riviera?
faq.answer: Typical driving times range from 3 hours to Dhermi and Jale, about 3.5 hours to Gjipe, and up to 4 hours for Ksamil or Sarandë. Times vary with traffic and weather, especially on the winding sections of the Llogara Pass.

faq.question: Which Albanian beaches are most easily accessed by rental car from Tirana?
faq.answer: The most accessible beaches are Dhermi, Jale, Gjipe, Ksamil and the Sarandë area. Each has a designated parking area close to the shoreline, and the routes are fully paved via the SH4/SH8 highways.

faq.question: What are the road conditions on the route from Tirana to the southern coast?
faq.answer: The main route (SH4 to SH8) is paved and generally in good condition, but it includes steep climbs, narrow bends, and occasional rockfalls near the Llogara Pass. Drive cautiously, especially in winter, and allow extra time for weather‑related delays.
